---
title: "21 OrgTree Readiness"
author: "UIAO Modernization Program"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
format:
  html: default
  docx: default
  pdf: default
  gfm: default
---

# OrgTree Readiness

**Document:** 21
**Phase:** 6 — OrgTree Readiness v0.6.0
**Version:** 1.0
**Status:** Draft
**Classification:** Public
**Date:** 2026-04-27

**Provenance:**
This document derives from and cites the following canonical sources:

| Canon ID | Title | ADR |
|---|---|---|
| UIAO_006 | AODIM Architecture | — |
| UIAO_007 | OrgTree Modernization — AD to Entra ID | — |
| ADR-035 | OrgPath Codebook — Executable Canon Binding | — |
| ADR-038 | Device-Plane OrgPath Provisioning | — |
| ADR-042 | AD Computer Conversion Guide — Phase 4 Device Planes | — |

All claims in this document trace to one of the above canonical sources.
See [00_CorePrinciples.qmd](00_CorePrinciples.qmd) for the governing SSOT principle.

---

## 1. Overview

The OrgTree Readiness pipeline resolves the structural mismatch between Active
Directory's X.500 container model (Organizational Units) and the cloud-native
attribute-driven model required by Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft Intune, and
Azure Arc.

Per **UIAO_007** (OrgTree Modernization), the core mental shift is:
> Stop thinking "tree of containers" — start thinking "tree of attributes +
> groups + delegation."

Per **UIAO_006** (AODIM), the governing identity principle is:
> Identity attributes define organizational structure; access is computed, not
> assigned.

The pipeline produces two readiness verdicts per device:

- **Intune readiness** — OS posture, TPM binding, HVCI, co-management state
- **Arc readiness** — network path, extension health, policy scope

Both verdicts are projected against the canonical `OrgPath` (per **ADR-035**)
and emitted as OSCAL evidence observations.

---

## 2. Inputs

| Input | Source | Canon Reference |
|---|---|---|
| AD computer object dump | AD Forest survey | ADR-042 |
| OrgPath codebook | `src/uiao/modernization/orgtree/` | ADR-035 |
| Intune device inventory | Intune Graph API adapter | ADR-038 |
| Arc registration status | Azure Arc ARM adapter | ADR-038 |
| KSI control library | `src/uiao/ksi/` | UIAO_002 |

The AD forest survey emits a SHA-256 content hash over all computer objects at
survey timestamp. This hash anchors the integrity chain (see Section 7).

---

## 3. Forest → Bundle Pipeline

The end-to-end pipeline moves from AD forest survey through OrgPath projection
to signed evidence bundle.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[AD Forest Survey\ncomputer-objects SHA-256] --> B[OrgPath Projection\ncodebook validation]
    B --> C{Codebook\nMatch?}
    C -- Yes --> D[Projected OrgPath\nattached to device record]
    C -- No --> E[Finding: DRIFT-PROVENANCE\nunmapped OU path]
    D --> F[Device-Plane Scan\nIntune + Arc adapters]
    F --> G[Readiness Findings\nper device]
    G --> H[Evidence Bundle\nbundle_hash + HMAC-SHA256]
    H --> I[OSCAL Emission\nobservations + findings]
    E --> H
```

**Pipeline invariants (per UIAO_002 §4):**

1. Each stage produces a content hash that chains to the next.
2. The evidence bundle is HMAC-SHA256 signed; any modification invalidates the
   signature.
3. OSCAL observations trace lineage back to the AD forest survey hash.

---

## 4. OrgPath Projection Model

OrgPath projection converts legacy OU distinguished names into the canonical
`orgPath` string hierarchy defined in **ADR-035**.

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    OU[AD OU Distinguished Name\ne.g. OU=IT,OU=Baltimore,OU=East] --> PROJ[OrgPath Projector\nsrc/uiao/modernization/orgtree/]
    PROJ --> CB{Codebook\nLookup}
    CB -- match --> VP[Validated OrgPath\nCORP/US/EAST/BALTIMORE/IT]
    CB -- no match --> UNK[Unmapped Path\nquarantined]
    VP --> DG[Dynamic Group Rule\nstartsWith / eq]
    VP --> AU[Administrative Unit\nscoped role assignment]
    VP --> CONF[Conformant\ndevice record]
    UNK --> FND[Finding emitted\nDRIFT-PROVENANCE]
```

**Codebook validation rules (ADR-035):**

- Exact-node match: `orgPath -eq "CORP/US/EAST/BALTIMORE/IT"`
- Branch match: `orgPath -startsWith "CORP/US/EAST"`
- Unresolvable paths are quarantined and a `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` finding is
  emitted before the record enters the device-plane scan.

---

## 5. Device-Plane Verdicts

### 5.1 Intune Readiness Decision Tree

Per **ADR-038**, Intune readiness gates on four sequential checks. A device is
`READY` only when all gates pass.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    START([Device record\n+ projected OrgPath]) --> OS{OS Version\nmeets baseline?}
    OS -- No --> FAIL_OS[FAIL: OS out of baseline\nfinding emitted]
    OS -- Yes --> TPM{TPM 2.0\npresent + active?}
    TPM -- No --> FAIL_TPM[FAIL: No TPM binding\nfinding emitted]
    TPM -- Yes --> HVCI{HVCI / Memory\nIntegrity enabled?}
    HVCI -- No --> WARN_HVCI[WARN: HVCI not enforced\nfinding emitted]
    HVCI -- Yes --> CO{Co-management\nstate enrolled?}
    CO -- No --> WARN_CO[WARN: Not co-managed\nfinding emitted]
    CO -- Yes --> INTUNE_READY([READY: Intune\nconformant])
```

### 5.2 Arc Readiness Decision Tree

Arc readiness gates on network reachability, extension health, and policy scope
alignment with the device's projected OrgPath (ADR-038).

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    START2([Device record\n+ projected OrgPath]) --> NET{Arc endpoint\nreachable?}
    NET -- No --> FAIL_NET[FAIL: Network path blocked\nfinding emitted]
    NET -- Yes --> EXT{Connected Machine\nagent healthy?}
    EXT -- No --> FAIL_EXT[FAIL: Agent unhealthy\nfinding emitted]
    EXT -- Yes --> POL{Policy scope\naligns OrgPath?}
    POL -- No --> WARN_POL[WARN: Policy scope mismatch\nfinding emitted]
    POL -- Yes --> ARC_READY([READY: Arc\nconformant])
```

---

## 6. Bundle Structure

The signed evidence bundle follows the UIAO_002 §4.4 pattern.

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `bundle_id` | URN | `urn:uiao:orgtree:{tenant}:{run_id}` |
| `survey_hash` | SHA-256 | Hash of AD computer object dump at survey time |
| `orgpath_version` | string | Codebook version from ADR-035 |
| `evidence_items` | list | One `EvidenceItem` per device verdict |
| `stable_hash` | SHA-256 | Content hash excluding volatile fields |
| `hmac_sig` | HMAC-SHA256 | Tamper-evident bundle signature |
| `timestamp` | ISO 8601 | Bundle creation time |

**Integrity chain diagram:** See [`docs/diagrams/uiao-orgtree-integrity-chain.puml`](../diagrams/uiao-orgtree-integrity-chain.puml)

The PlantUML source above maps to the UIAO_002 §4 four-plane pattern:

1. Survey → content hash
2. Projection + device scan → readiness findings
3. Evidence bundle → HMAC-SHA256 signature
4. OSCAL emission → lineage trace back to survey hash

---

## 7. OSCAL Emission

Each device verdict produces an OSCAL 1.1.2 observation:

```yaml
observation:
  uuid: <deterministic from evidence_item hash>
  title: "OrgTree Readiness — <device_id>"
  description: "Intune: <verdict> | Arc: <verdict>"
  methods:
    - AUTOMATED
  relevant-evidence:
    - href: <bundle_id>
      description: "Signed OrgTree readiness bundle"
  props:
    - name: survey-hash
      value: <survey_hash>
    - name: orgpath
      value: <projected_orgpath>
    - name: canon-ref
      value: "UIAO_007 / ADR-035 / ADR-038"
```

Failed or warned devices generate a corresponding OSCAL finding with remediation
state `DETECTED → OPEN`.

---

## 8. KSI Coverage

| KSI ID | KSI Description | Gate |
|---|---|---|
| KSI-DEV-01 | Device OS baseline compliance | Intune OS gate |
| KSI-DEV-02 | TPM 2.0 binding present | Intune TPM gate |
| KSI-DEV-03 | Memory integrity (HVCI) enforced | Intune HVCI gate |
| KSI-DEV-04 | Co-management enrollment | Intune co-mgmt gate |
| KSI-ARC-01 | Arc agent reachability | Arc network gate |
| KSI-ARC-02 | Connected Machine agent health | Arc extension gate |
| KSI-ID-05 | OrgPath codebook alignment | Projection codebook gate |

KSI evaluation follows the conservative aggregation rule from UIAO_002 §4.6:
if any constituent check FAILS, the aggregate KSI status is FAIL; if any WARNS,
the status is WARN; otherwise PASS.

---

## References

- [UIAO_006 — AODIM Architecture](../../src/uiao/canon/UIAO_006_AODIM_Architecture_v1.0.md)
- [UIAO_007 — OrgTree Modernization](../../src/uiao/canon/UIAO_007_OrgTree_Modernization_AD_to_EntraID_v1.0.md)
- [ADR-035 — OrgPath Codebook](../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-035-orgpath-codebook-binding.md)
- [ADR-038 — Device-Plane OrgPath](../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-038-device-plane-orgpath.md)
- [ADR-042 — AD Computer Conversion](../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-042-ad-computer-conversion-guide-integration.md)
- [00_CorePrinciples.qmd](00_CorePrinciples.qmd)
- [PlantUML integrity chain](../diagrams/uiao-orgtree-integrity-chain.puml)
